https://pbs.twimg.com/media/EpcfEYdW8AIDkBn.jpgWe aim to develop a novel technique, ProteoPicking , leveraging 
the advantages of t-CycIF and MS-based proteomics . This 
method has the potential to unveil structural, functional, and 
PTM proteomic landscapes within the tumor microenvironment 
with an unprecedented spatial precision .
https://pbs.twimg.com/media/EpcfEYdW8AIDkBn.jpgProteoPicking : bridging t -CycIF with precision tissue 
microdissection for TME proteomic exploration
Lina Maltrovsky1, Ada Junquera1, Saundarya Shah1, Matilda Salko1, Anni Härkkonen1 2, Maija Väärikoski1 2,
Ulla -Maija Haltia1 2, Otto Kauko , Anniina Färkkilä 1 2 4

•t-CycIF does not affect the number of proteins in the tissue in comparison to

•Proteomics of tumor and stroma cells differ from one another – the average 
amount of identified proteins in the tumor is higher than in the stroma
•The number of picks correlates with the number of identified proteins
1. Optimizing the method by
- reducing the amount of impurities in the samples
- reducing number of necessary picks
2. Creating a pipeline for the effective analysis of the proteomics raw data
- Differential Protein Expression Analysis
- Pathway Enrichment Analysis
3. Comparing proteomics of tumor, tumor -stroma interface (TSI), and 
stroma 
4. Comparing proteomics of proliferating cells with senescent 
cells (p21 high Ki67– pRB–) 
5. Mapping picking sites on the t-CycIF images

•Analyses proteins on a large scale
•Not panel -based
•Information on structure, functions, roles, 
& post translational modifications (PTM)
•Enables insight into cellular processes &
